title: DialerAuth: A Motion-assisted Touch-based Smartphone User Authentication Scheme
venue: Proceedings of Eighth ACM Conference on Data and Application Security and Privacy (CODASPY’18)

method.input_representation: Fused feature vector of 70 features (32 inertial features, 38 timing-based features)

evaluation.context: Derived from the best TAR of 85.77% achieved in the sitting activity using MLP with 9 hidden layers
Achieved in the walking downstairs activity using MLP with 4 hidden layers

evaluation_protocol: Owner vs. all non-owners (one-class classification) across four different physical postures

reported_limitations: Overall performance is lower than fixed-text keystroke dynamics
Requires a relatively large amount of training patterns (30 samples)

usability.usability_issues: Some users found the enrollment/training process (30 patterns) cumbersome

key_contribution: Proposed a bimodal system using text-independent dialing behavior and inertial micro-movements
Evaluated system across multiple postures in an unsupervised field setting
main_strength: Large-scale dataset for behavioral biometrics (97 users)
Includes a comprehensive usability study using SUS
main_weakness: Lower accuracy compared to state-of-the-art fixed-text schemes
insights.notes_for_review: The system is designed for 'one-shot' authentication at login, though the authors suggest potential for continuous authentication in future work.
